title: BTS Opens North America Leg at Raymond James Stadium — ARIRANG World Tour Arrives in Tampa
detail: On April 25, 2026, BTS opens the North American leg of the ARIRANG World Tour at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida — the first time all seven members have headlined a US stadium together since 2019. The show begins at 8:00 PM ET; gates opened at 5:30 PM with no opening act. Approximately 60,000–65,000 fans fill the venue on opening night, the first of three sold-out Tampa dates (April 25, 26, and 28, ~180,000 total). The setlist spans 23 songs, opening with 'Hooligan,' 'Aliens,' and 'Run BTS (Remix)' before moving through ARIRANG tracks including 'Please,' 'Into the Sun,' 'SWIM,' 'Like Animals,' and 'Merry Go Round,' alongside fan-favorite hits 'FAKE LOVE,' 'MIC Drop,' 'IDOL,' 'Butter,' and 'Dynamite.' Two rotating 'surprise song' slots conclude the night. Tampa Bay Boulevard remains closed for the third consecutive day for merchandise operations. US media coverage describes scenes of fans from across North America descending on the city as comparable to Beatlemania. The Tampa run represents the largest K-pop concert event ever held in Florida.

media.caption: BTS opens the ARIRANG World Tour North America leg at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, FL on April 25, 2026

title: 'SWIM' Leads Billboard Global 200 for Fourth Consecutive Week — BTS's Record-Extending 8th Chart-Topper
detail: On the Billboard Global 200 and Billboard Global Excl. U.S. charts dated April 25, 2026, BTS's 'SWIM' secures a fourth consecutive week at No. 1 — becoming BTS's record-extending eighth leader on each global survey. The single registered 73 million global streams that week (63.4 million outside the U.S.) and 22,000 downloads worldwide. On the Billboard Hot 100, 'SWIM' holds at No. 10 for its fourth chart week, making it BTS's longest-running top-10 Hot 100 entry since 'Butter' in 2021. The chart dominance coincides with the opening night of the ARIRANG World Tour North America leg in Tampa.

media.caption: 'SWIM' tops Billboard Global 200 for a fourth consecutive week as BTS opens their North America tour in Tampa
